title: Ebola Crisis Worsens as Death Toll Passes 200
subtitle: Health officials struggle to contain the virus as international funding remains slow to arrive

summary.en: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has reached a grim new milestone. Confirmed deaths from the virus have now surpassed 200 as cases continue to rise rapidly. Health experts report that infection numbers grew by nearly 40 percent in just one week. While the U.S. government has promised emergency funds, international support is currently falling short. Experts are calling for faster action to stop the spread of this dangerous disease.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國的伊波拉疫情已達到一個嚴峻的新里程碑。隨著病例持續快速增加，該病毒的確診死亡人數現已突破兩百人。衛生專家報告指出，感染人數在短短一週內成長了近百分之四十。儘管美國政府已承諾提供緊急資金，但國際支援目前仍顯不足。專家呼籲採取更迅速的行動，以阻止這種危險疾病的蔓延。

paragraphs.en: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is moving into a more dangerous phase. According to MedPage Today, the number of confirmed deaths has now passed 200. This is a significant increase from previous reports, highlighting the speed at which the virus is moving through local communities. Health workers are working under extreme pressure to treat the sick and stop the virus from spreading further, but the situation remains very difficult.
paragraphs.zh: 剛果民主共和國的伊波拉病毒疫情正進入更危險的階段。根據《今日醫學新聞》(MedPage Today) 報導，確診死亡人數現已超過兩百人。這比先前的報告有顯著增加，凸顯了病毒在當地社區傳播的速度。醫護人員正承受極大壓力，試圖治療病患並阻止病毒進一步擴散，但目前情況依然十分艱困。

paragraphs.en: Recent data shows that the number of Ebola cases has jumped by almost 40 percent in just one week. This rapid rise is causing alarm among international health experts. The virus, which causes severe bleeding and fever, is often fatal if not treated quickly. As the number of patients grows, medical teams are facing shortages of supplies and space, making it harder to provide the necessary care to those who are infected.
paragraphs.zh: 最新數據顯示，伊波拉病例數在短短一週內激增了近百分之四十。這種快速上升引起了國際衛生專家的警覺。這種病毒會導致嚴重出血與發燒，若未及時治療，往往致命。隨著病患人數增加，醫療團隊面臨物資與空間短缺，使得為感染者提供必要照護變得更加困難。

paragraphs.en: In response to the growing crisis, the United States Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has activated $107 million in emergency funding. This money is intended to support the international response to the outbreak and help local health systems manage the surge in patients. However, there is a major gap between the money promised and the money actually available on the ground. The Africa CDC noted that less than 10 percent of the total money promised by international donors has been received so far. This delay in funding is a major concern for those working on the front lines of the response.
paragraphs.zh: 為應對日益嚴峻的危機，美國疾病管制與預防中心 (CDC) 已啟動一億零七百萬美元的緊急資金。這筆款項旨在支持國際間對疫情的反應，並協助當地醫療體系應對病患激增的問題。然而，承諾的資金與實際到位資金之間存在巨大落差。非洲疾病管制與預防中心指出，目前收到的國際捐助資金不到承諾總額的百分之十。資金撥付的延遲，讓站在防疫第一線的工作人員感到憂心。

paragraphs.en: Public health officials are worried that the lack of immediate funding will make it impossible to control the outbreak before it spreads even further. Without enough money, it is difficult to buy medicine, pay for medical staff, and set up the safe treatment centers needed to isolate patients. The situation is further complicated by the nature of the virus, which requires strict safety measures to protect healthcare workers. When these measures are not fully supported by adequate resources, the risk of the virus spreading to those trying to help becomes much higher.
paragraphs.zh: 公共衛生官員擔心，缺乏即時資金將使疫情在進一步擴散前無法受到控制。若沒有足夠的經費，將難以購買藥品、支付醫護人員薪資，並建立隔離病患所需的安全治療中心。病毒本身的特性使情況更加複雜，因為這需要嚴格的安全措施來保護醫護人員。當這些措施缺乏足夠資源支持時，病毒傳染給救援人員的風險就會大幅提高。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the immediate medical needs, the outbreak is having a deep impact on the stability of the region. Families are being torn apart, and the fear of the disease is causing many to avoid seeking help at hospitals. This behavior can make the outbreak harder to track and control. International organizations are urging countries to move faster to fulfill their financial promises. They argue that the cost of waiting will be much higher in the long run, both in terms of human lives and the economic impact on the region.
paragraphs.zh: 除了迫切的醫療需求外，疫情也對該地區的穩定造成深遠影響。許多家庭破碎，且對疾病的恐懼導致許多人不敢前往醫院求助。這種行為可能使疫情更難以追蹤與控制。國際組織正敦促各國加快腳步，履行其財務承諾。他們認為，從長遠來看，等待的代價將會更高，無論是在人命損失還是對該地區的經濟衝擊方面皆然。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the outlook remains uncertain. While the international community is aware of the severity of the situation, the slow pace of support is a major obstacle. Experts suggest that if the current trend continues, the death toll will likely rise significantly in the coming weeks. The focus for now remains on providing essential care to the sick and ensuring that health workers have the protection they need to do their jobs safely. The global community is watching closely, hoping that more support will arrive soon to help bring this deadly outbreak to an end.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，前景依然不明朗。雖然國際社會已意識到情況的嚴重性，但支援速度緩慢是一大阻礙。專家指出，若目前的趨勢持續下去，未來幾週的死亡人數可能會顯著攀升。目前的重點仍是為病患提供基本照護，並確保醫護人員擁有安全執行任務所需的防護。全球社會正密切關注，期盼更多支援能儘快到位，以協助終結這場致命的疫情。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase suddenly and by a large amount.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「跳躍」，這裡指數據的急劇上升。文中：Recent data shows that the number of Ebola cases has jumped by almost 40 percent in just one week.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A difference between two things, especially between what is promised and what is actually available.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 差距、缺口（指預期與現實的落差）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指承諾金額與實際到帳金額的落差。文中：However, there is a major gap between the money promised and the money actually available on the ground.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause a group or family to be separated or destroyed by conflict or distress.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡形容家庭因疫情而被迫分離。文中：Families are being torn apart, and the fear of the disease is causing many to avoid seeking help at hospitals.

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: Outbreak Kills Over 200; ACIP Investigation Launched; Hackers Demand $75M From Novo

previousArticles.title: Ebola Crisis Deepens as Death Toll Passes 200
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is worsening quickly. Official reports show that the number of deaths has now passed 200. Cases have increased by nearly 40 percent in just one week. While the U.S. CDC has provided emergency funding, international aid is arriving slowly. Experts warn that more support is needed to stop the virus from spreading further.

previousArticles.title: Ebola Outbreak Grows as Health Officials Call for Urgent Support
previousArticles.summaryEn: A serious Ebola outbreak is spreading across the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. Health officials report that confirmed cases have increased by nearly 40 percent in just one week. The disease is caused by a rare virus that currently has no approved vaccine. International organizations are now providing emergency funding to help stop the spread. However, experts warn that more money and personnel are required to manage the crisis effectively.

previousArticles.title: Global Response Grows as Ebola Outbreak Spreads in the DRC
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is facing a serious Ebola outbreak that has already caused hundreds of deaths. International leaders at the G7 summit have pledged significant financial support to help contain the virus. The current outbreak involves a rare strain that is difficult to treat with existing medical tools. Local communities in Bunia are struggling with both the health risks and the economic impact of the disease. Experts warn that global cooperation is essential to manage this public health emergency effectively.

quiz.question: According to the Africa CDC, what is the current status of the international financial aid promised for the Ebola outbreak?

options.A: The full $107 million has been successfully distributed to local hospitals.
options.B: Less than 10 percent of the pledged funds have actually reached the response efforts.
options.C: The funding has been delayed because the CDC has not yet activated the emergency budget.
options.D: Most of the money has been used to pay for medical staff salaries.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出「the Africa CDC noted that less than 10 percent of the total money promised by international donors has been received so far」，這說明資金到位率極低。選項 A 與文中提到的「major gap」矛盾；選項 C 錯誤，因為 CDC 已經啟動了資金；選項 D 文中並未提到資金具體用於支付薪水的比例，且與資金尚未到位的事實不符。

quiz.question: Why does the fear of Ebola among local families contribute to the difficulty of controlling the outbreak?

options.A: It forces families to spend all their savings on private medical treatments.
options.B: It leads to a shortage of safety equipment for international health workers.
options.C: It discourages people from going to hospitals, making it harder for experts to track and manage the virus.
options.D: It causes families to migrate to other regions, spreading the virus to unaffected areas.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，文章提到「the fear of the disease is causing many to avoid seeking help at hospitals. This behavior can make the outbreak harder to track and control」。這表示人們因恐懼而避開醫療體系，導致衛生單位難以掌握疫情動態。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 B 是資源問題，與民眾行為無直接因果關係；選項 D 文中並未討論民眾遷移導致擴散，而是強調醫院追蹤困難。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'torn' mean in the phrase 'Families are being torn apart'?

options.A: Physically damaged or ripped into pieces.
options.B: Separated or broken up due to external pressures or tragedy.
options.C: Confused and unable to make decisions.
options.D: Forced to move to a different location against their will.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境中，「torn apart」並非字面上的撕裂，而是比喻家庭因疫情造成的死亡、隔離或恐懼而破碎、分離。選項 A 是字面義，不適用於家庭關係；選項 C 與「torn」的隱喻用法無關；選項 D 雖然可能發生，但「torn apart」主要強調的是家庭結構的崩解與情感連結的中斷，而非單純的遷移。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the Ebola outbreak in the Congo?

options.A: The virus is becoming less dangerous as more international experts arrive to help.
options.B: Local communities are solely responsible for failing to manage the virus effectively.
options.C: The outbreak is a serious, escalating crisis hindered by a lack of timely international financial support.
options.D: The CDC is the only organization capable of stopping the virus from spreading further.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於描述疫情的嚴重性（死亡人數增加、擴散迅速）以及國際援助遲緩帶來的困境（資金不到位、資源短缺）。選項 A 與文意相反，疫情正在惡化；選項 B 將責任歸咎於當地社區，與文中強調國際資金延遲的觀點不符；選項 D 過於誇大，文中提到多個組織參與，並未將功勞或責任全歸於 CDC。
